prerequisites: Maintenance window scheduled — upgrade causes a full control/data plane outage unless warm reboot is used post-install
All critical containers healthy: docker ps | grep -E 'database|swss|syncd|bgp'
Sufficient disk space on /host (>2GB free: df -h /host)
Running configuration saved to disk: ls -la /etc/sonic/config_db.json
Pre-upgrade baseline captured: BGP peer count, route table size, interface states
New image URL or local file path available and accessible from the switch
Out-of-band (OOB) management access confirmed in case in-band access is lost post-reboot
purpose: Upgrade SONiC OS image to a new version with rollback capability

rollback.action: Cancel upgrade before rebooting — revert next boot to current image
rollback.notes: If you have not yet rebooted, simply reset the default boot image to the currently running version. Use the image name from 'sonic-installer list'. The new image remains installed on disk and can be activated later.

rollback.action: After booting new image: set old image as default and reboot back

commands.command: sudo sonic-installer set-default <old-image-name>; sudo config save -y; sudo reboot

rollback.notes: If the new image fails (containers won't start, config migration broke, BGP unrecoverable), roll back by setting the old image as default and rebooting. The old image slot is preserved as long as disk space allowed dual-image layout. Verify old image is still listed: sonic-installer list.

rollback.action: If sonic-installer rollback is available, use it as a shortcut

commands.command: sudo sonic-installer rollback; sudo config save -y; sudo reboot

rollback.notes: Some SONiC builds include 'sonic-installer rollback' which atomically sets the previous image as default. Check availability: sonic-installer --help | grep rollback. If not available, use rollback-2 manually. After rollback boot, verify CONFIG_DB loaded correctly — schema migrations may not be reversible and config reload may be required.

rollback.action: If config_db.json was migrated and is incompatible after rollback, restore from backup
rollback.notes: If the rolled-back image fails to load config_db.json due to forward-only schema migration, restore the pre-upgrade backup (captured at step-3 equivalent: sudo sonic-cfggen -d --print-data > /tmp/pre_upgrade_config_backup.json). Then reload: 'sudo config reload -y'. This triggers a full config pipeline replay through CONFIG_DB -> orchagent -> syncd.

steps.action: Verify current image version and available disk space
steps.notes: sonic-installer list shows both image slots and which is current/next. If only one slot is used and disk is tight, the old image will be removed automatically on install — this eliminates the fallback slot.

verification.command: sonic-installer list; df /host | awk 'NR==2 {avail=$4; unit=substr($4,length($4)); if (avail+0 > 2097152) print "OK: " avail " free"; else print "WARN: insufficient space"}'
verification.confirms_if: Disk has enough space for the new image and current image slot is known
verification.look_for: Current image identified and at least 2GB free on /host

commands.command: show bgp summary; show ip route summary; show interfaces status | grep -v 'admin down'

steps.notes: Save this output: show bgp summary > /tmp/pre_upgrade_bgp.txt && show ip route summary > /tmp/pre_upgrade_routes.txt. Compare after upgrade to confirm full recovery.

verification.command: show bgp summary | grep -c Established; show ip route summary | grep -E 'Total|connected|bgp'; show interfaces status | grep -c ' up '
verification.confirms_if: Baseline numbers captured; discrepancies after upgrade are immediately detectable
verification.look_for: BGP peer count, total route count, and up interface count recorded for post-upgrade comparison

daemon_impact.impact: CONFIG_DB read to disk — no service interruption

steps.notes: config save -y writes CONFIG_DB (db 4) to /etc/sonic/config_db.json. This file is the source of truth on next boot. Verify it is not empty or zero-length before proceeding.

verification.confirms_if: Running CONFIG_DB state is persisted and will be loaded by the new image on boot
verification.look_for: config_db.json exists, is non-zero, and parses as valid JSON

steps.action: Download or copy the new SONiC image to the switch
steps.notes: Accepts HTTP/HTTPS URLs or local file paths (e.g., /tmp/sonic-vs.bin). The installer writes to /host/image-<version>/. If disk space is insufficient mid-install, the boot partition may be left in a partially written state — verify with 'sonic-installer list' before proceeding. To pre-stage the file: curl -o /tmp/sonic.bin <url> then run sonic-installer install /tmp/sonic.bin.

verification.confirms_if: Install wrote the image to a boot slot without errors
verification.look_for: New image appears in the list with the correct version string

steps.action: Verify new image installed and identify its slot name
steps.notes: Note the exact image name string (e.g., 'SONiC-OS-202511.1234-generic'). This is required for the set-next-boot and rollback commands. If the new image does not appear, check /host for partial files and re-run the install step.

verification.confirms_if: Image is present in /host and indexed by sonic-installer
verification.look_for: New image version appears as a non-current entry

steps.action: Set new image as the next boot target
steps.notes: This command writes to the GRUB configuration. It does NOT reboot. Use the exact image name string from step-5. After this step, if you decide not to upgrade, run 'sudo sonic-installer set-default <current-image>' to undo.

verification.confirms_if: GRUB/bootloader will load the new image on next reboot
verification.look_for: Next boot entry points to the new image version

steps.action: Perform final config save and initiate reboot

daemon_impact.impact: Stopped — full data plane outage begins
CONFIG_DB flushed to disk before shutdown

steps.notes: The second config save guards against any CONFIG_DB writes that occurred between step-3 and now (e.g., interface flaps updating STATE_DB that bled into CONFIG_DB). Expect 3-5 minutes of downtime for a typical switch. Monitor reachability from OOB management. Verify: Switch powers down and begins boot sequence. SSH session will drop. (confirms if Reboot initiated; switch will come up on new image)

steps.action: Verify new image version after reboot
steps.notes: If version does not match, GRUB may have fallen back to the old image due to a failed boot count check. Run 'show reboot-cause' to determine why the expected image was not loaded.

verification.command: show version | grep -E 'SONiC Software Version|Build Date'
verification.confirms_if: Switch booted from the correct image slot
verification.look_for: Version string matches the newly installed image

steps.action: Verify all critical containers are running

daemon_impact.impact: Should be running and reconciling CONFIG_DB to APPL_DB
Should be running and programming ASIC via SAI

steps.notes: If containers are stuck in restarting state: 'docker logs <container> --tail 50' to diagnose. A CONFIG_DB schema migration failure is the most common cause on cross-version upgrades. Check journalctl -u sonic.target for systemd-level failures.

verification.confirms_if: Container startup completed and management plane is functional
verification.look_for: All critical containers in running state (count >= 6)

steps.action: Verify BGP sessions, routes, and interfaces match pre-upgrade baseline

commands.command: show bgp summary; show ip route summary; show interfaces status

steps.notes: Allow up to 5 minutes for BGP sessions to re-establish and routes to reconverge after boot. If BGP count is lower than baseline, check 'show bgp neighbors <peer> | grep -i state' and 'show log | grep bgpd'. If routes are missing, check 'show ip route' and orchagent logs: 'docker exec -it swss cat /var/log/swss/orchagent.log | tail -100'.

verification.command: show bgp summary | grep -c Established; show ip route summary | grep Total; show interfaces status | grep -c ' up '
verification.confirms_if: Full forwarding plane recovery confirmed; upgrade successful
verification.look_for: BGP peer count, route total, and up interface count match pre-upgrade baseline from step-2

warnings: A full reboot is required after sonic-installer install — the data plane will be down for the full boot cycle (typically 3-5 minutes).
SONiC dual-image layout keeps current and new image. If /host has <2GB free, the install will fail mid-write.
CONFIG_DB schema migrations are one-way. Rollback to the old image may restore the binary but config_db.json may have been migrated. Test config reload after rollback.
Do NOT interrupt power or connectivity during the install step — partial writes can corrupt the boot partition.
After setting next boot image, always run 'config save -y' before rebooting to ensure the latest CONFIG_DB state is persisted to /etc/sonic/config_db.json.
sonic-installer rollback is only available in some SONiC versions and may not be present in community builds — verify before relying on it.
Cross-major-version upgrades (e.g., 202205 to 202511) may introduce breaking CONFIG_DB schema changes. Review release notes before upgrading.
